strengths: Three objects are clearly situated in specific real-world contexts, demonstrating a solid grasp of how bias manifests in diverse settings.
The commentary includes creative and coherent links to TOK concepts (e.g., finite digit storage as statistical bias) that go beyond mere description.
The exhibition prompt is precisely stated and consistently referenced, ensuring strong alignment with the IA requirement.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven: the gavel is treated symbolically rather than as a concrete artefact with unique provenance.
Citation and referencing are inconsistent and incomplete, reducing the academic rigor of the justifications.
Key claims (e.g., inevitability of herd conformity, compounding rounding error) are asserted without sufficient TOK theory or precise sourcing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition fulfills the descriptors for the 5â6 band by identifying three well-contextualized objects and providing clear links to the chosen prompt on bias. It also shows emerging features of the 7â8 band: most points are supported by evidence and the contributions of each object are effectively justified. However, lapses in specificity (especially regarding the gavel artefact), uneven depth of analysis, and inconsistent citations prevent a fully sustained, rigorous treatment at the higher band. Placing the work at the low end of band 7â8 accurately reflects these strengths and limitations, justifying a final score of 7.

annotations.comment: Ensure each citation includes all required information (author, title in italics, publisher, date) and follows a consistent style.
The prompt is clearly stated, providing a precise basis for the objectsâ analysis and ensuring alignment with IA prompt #12.
The image appears to be a generic journal cover rather than a specific Chinese history textbook; selecting a photograph of the actual textbook would strengthen the objectâs authenticity.
The student situates the object within the clear realâworld context of the One China Policy, effectively demonstrating how power dynamics shape knowledge production.
The concept of âherd biasâ is introduced but lacks a precise connection to TOK terminology or theory; consider defining it using Theory of Knowledge concepts such as shared knowledge structures.
The final paragraph reiterates earlier points; consolidating similar ideas would improve clarity and avoid repetition in the justification for this object.
The caption cites Zhang Jieâs journal but the analysis references a textbook; could the student clarify the relationship between the image source and the described object?
The student could enhance the analysis by identifying the relevant Ways of Knowing or Areas of Knowledge to frame how political bias in textbooks functions within TOK.
The gavel image is generic; procuring a photograph from the specific Larry Nassar trial would reinforce the objectâs realâworld context.
The differentiation between judicial and emotional bias is insightful and shows nuanced understanding of how bias operates in legal knowledge.
The introduction of the gavelâs role lacks TOK vocabulary such as WOKs or KFs; integrating these terms would deepen the TOK analysis.
Consider explicitly linking this discussion to the knowledge framework for law to show how bias in precedent fits within TOK structures.
Providing a citation or source for Judge Aquilinaâs statement would strengthen the evidence base for the emotional bias example.
The gavel is treated symbolically rather than as a unique artefact; giving more specific provenance would enrich the objectâs justification.
How does the distinction between judicial and emotional bias affect our understanding of knowledge production in the legal AOK?
Identifying the calculator as a producer of knowledge via finite digit storage is a creative link between technology and statistical bias.
The explanation would benefit from a concrete numerical example (e.g., storing 15 digits of $$\pi$$) to illustrate rounding bias more vividly.
Use mathematical notation (e.g., $$\pi=3.1415\ldots$$) to clarify the discussion of finite digit storage and enhance academic precision.
The leap from rounding error to inevitable bias is asserted rather than examined; consider exploring conditions under which rounding bias substantially affects results.
Integrate TOK concepts such as âcertaintyâ and âerrorâ explicitly to tie the technical discussion back to the exhibitionâs central theme.
The works cited header is present, but the reference list lacks consistent formatting and appears incomplete.
This reference entry uses quotation marks inconsistently and omits the access date; standardize punctuation and include complete details.
The article title is incomplete (missing closing quotation mark) and lacks journal details; revise to include full bibliographic information.
Alphabetize the works cited list by author surname to adhere to standard academic conventions.
Use hanging indent formatting for URLs to improve readability and visual consistency in the reference list.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
